sessions.title: 30 sec jog / 2 min walk x 8
sessions.purpose: Bridge into July by keeping the strength work and introducing more controlled run/walk.
coachingNotes: This is the first week with running exposure. The run/walk only counts as progress if joints and calves stay settled the next day.
If the jog/walk causes next-day pain above 4/10, repeat this exact week before advancing.
blocks: If the jog/walk causes next-day pain above 4/10, repeat this exact week before advancing.

sessions.title: 1 min jog / 2 min walk x 8
sessions.purpose: Daily walk logging opens. Walk daily, then layer intervals, strength, and one longer hike on top.
coachingNotes: The win this week is steady form and repeatability, not a harder run/walk effort.
If Wednesday feels clunky, repeat Level 2 next week instead of moving on automatically.
blocks: If Wednesday feels clunky, repeat Level 2 next week instead of moving on automatically.

sessions.title: 1 min jog / 2 min walk x 10
sessions.purpose: Keep the walk base steady, add a second quality session, and protect recovery.
coachingNotes: This week starts to feel like training rather than just restarting. Respect that by keeping the easy work truly easy.
If the hike trashes the next two days, reduce the next long day by 15-20 percent.
blocks: If the hike trashes the next two days, reduce the next long day by 15-20 percent.

sessions.title: 90 sec jog / 2 min walk x 8
sessions.purpose: Add hill-strength support and a longer hike, keeping the walk habit and recovery in order.
coachingNotes: This is the first week where hill strength and long-day demand start to meet. Keep form high and ego low.
If Wednesday or Saturday pushes joint signal to 5+, back the next run/walk down a level.
blocks: If Wednesday or Saturday pushes joint signal to 5+, back the next run/walk down a level.

sessions.title: Easy 20 min run/walk
sessions.purpose: Take an easier week, absorb the load, and keep the walking base simple.
coachingNotes: Easier weeks are part of the plan, not a reward for good behavior. Let this one absorb the last three weeks.
If you feel great, keep the plan unchanged anyway.
blocks: If you feel great, keep the plan unchanged anyway.

sessions.title: 2 min jog / 2 min walk x 8
sessions.purpose: Normalise longer time on feet while maintaining strength work.
coachingNotes: Endurance foundation begins here. The body should start seeing longer trail time as normal rather than exceptional.
If Saturday becomes a grind, hold 15 km again next week rather than pushing distance.
blocks: If Saturday becomes a grind, hold 15 km again next week rather than pushing distance.

sessions.title: 30 min easy run/walk
sessions.purpose: Keep hikes growing and stay patient.
coachingNotes: This week is about accumulating sensible work, not one standout hero session.
If lower back or hips take over from legs, reduce the next hill-strength load slightly.
blocks: If lower back or hips take over from legs, reduce the next hill-strength load slightly.

sessions.title: 35 min easy run/walk
sessions.purpose: Blend hill walking with a long hike.
coachingNotes: You are starting to build a real long-day engine now. The key is still restraint early in the hike.
If descents push pain above 4/10, find gentler terrain next week and strengthen downhill-specific control.
blocks: If descents push pain above 4/10, find gentler terrain next week and strengthen downhill-specific control.

sessions.title: Easy 25 min run/walk
sessions.purpose: Bank an easier week before September.
coachingNotes: This week should feel easier before it feels impressive. That is exactly the point.
If you still feel cooked, strip Wednesday back to walking only.
blocks: If you still feel cooked, strip Wednesday back to walking only.

sessions.title: 40 min run/walk
sessions.purpose: Step into bigger trail-specific volume.
coachingNotes: This is the first week that should start feeling close to real event preparation. Strong pacing matters more than bravado.
If Saturday wipes you out for more than 48 hours, hold this level before progressing.
blocks: If Saturday wipes you out for more than 48 hours, hold this level before progressing.

sessions.title: 45 min run/walk
sessions.purpose: Practice a longer trail-style outing.
coachingNotes: Trail confidence is a skill. Use this week to become calmer on uneven ground rather than faster.
If footing makes you tense, repeat similar terrain before increasing distance.
blocks: If footing makes you tense, repeat similar terrain before increasing distance.

sessions.title: 45-50 min run/walk
sessions.purpose: Bring hill strength into a 22-25 km day.
coachingNotes: The big goal is still durability. A long day only helps if you can recover from it in a reasonable window.
If you finish shattered, reduce the next long day and improve fueling first.
blocks: If you finish shattered, reduce the next long day and improve fueling first.

sessions.title: Easy 30 min run/walk
sessions.purpose: Ease back to protect the build.
coachingNotes: This is another absorb-and-reset week. Protect it so the next block lands well.
If you still feel beat up, turn Saturday into a flatter walk and keep the week truly easy.
blocks: If you still feel beat up, turn Saturday into a flatter walk and keep the week truly easy.

sessions.title: 8-10 km run/walk
sessions.purpose: Push the long hike without making running the hero.
coachingNotes: The half marathon build starts to matter here, but hiking durability still has equal billing.
If run volume hurts recovery for Saturday, trim the Wednesday distance rather than the hike.
blocks: If run volume hurts recovery for Saturday, trim the Wednesday distance rather than the hike.

sessions.title: 9-11 km run/walk
sessions.purpose: Keep legs durable while volume stays high.
coachingNotes: The combination of longer run/walk and long mixed trail day is the point this week. Keep both sub-maximal.
If Wednesday bleeds into Saturday, shorten the run/walk before cutting the trail-specific work.
blocks: If Wednesday bleeds into Saturday, shorten the run/walk before cutting the trail-specific work.

sessions.title: 10-12 km run/walk
sessions.purpose: Peak hiking durability before the event taper.
coachingNotes: This is one of the heaviest trail weeks before the half. Pace matters less than how you manage the whole day.
If you are wrecked for more than two days, reduce peak volume before adding more running.
blocks: If you are wrecked for more than two days, reduce peak volume before adding more running.

sessions.title: 14-18 km run/walk
sessions.purpose: Peak the half build without chasing pace.
coachingNotes: This is the peak half-marathon week, but the route challenge still matters. That means restraint, not racing training.
If the Wednesday effort gets too hard, stop early and treat it as enough for the week.
blocks: If the Wednesday effort gets too hard, stop early and treat it as enough for the week.

sessions.title: Easy 25-30 min run/walk
sessions.purpose: Arrive calm and finish safely.
coachingNotes: The event is a checkpoint. A smart, even run/walk finish is more valuable than a messy overperformance.
If anything feels wrong on the day, convert the event fully to run/walk and protect the bigger winter goal.
blocks: If anything feels wrong on the day, convert the event fully to run/walk and protect the bigger winter goal.

sessions.title: Half marathon run/walk
sessions.purpose: Arrive calm and finish safely.
coachingNotes: The event is a checkpoint. A smart, even run/walk finish is more valuable than a messy overperformance.
If anything feels wrong on the day, convert the event fully to run/walk and protect the bigger winter goal.
blocks: If anything feels wrong on the day, convert the event fully to run/walk and protect the bigger winter goal.

sessions.title: Easy 40 min run/walk
sessions.purpose: Return to trail-specific structure.
coachingNotes: This is the pivot back to the real target. The half is done; now everything points toward winter trail durability.
If recovery is still sluggish from the half, cut the run/walk first, not the trail day.
blocks: If recovery is still sluggish from the half, cut the run/walk first, not the trail day.

sessions.title: 8-10 km run/walk
sessions.purpose: Hit the key 30-35 km benchmark week.
coachingNotes: This is the benchmark week. The goal is not speed. The goal is proving you can manage a long day and still think clearly.
If conditions or body state are poor, shorten the day intelligently and reschedule rather than forcing it.
blocks: If conditions or body state are poor, shorten the day intelligently and reschedule rather than forcing it.

sessions.title: Easy 30 min run/walk
sessions.purpose: Recover before the last specific push.
coachingNotes: Absorb the biggest training week. This is where you turn one huge effort into usable adaptation.
If still trashed, strip Wednesday down to walking and keep the long day at the short end.
blocks: If still trashed, strip Wednesday down to walking and keep the long day at the short end.

sessions.title: 8-10 km run/walk
sessions.purpose: Rehearse with kit and purposeful terrain.
coachingNotes: This is the last specific week. It should increase confidence, not create new fitness at all costs.
If the long rehearsal exposes a problem, solve the problem - do not cram more mileage.
blocks: If the long rehearsal exposes a problem, solve the problem - do not cram more mileage.

sessions.title: Easy 30-40 min run/walk
sessions.purpose: Reduce fatigue without going flat.
coachingNotes: The taper is about arriving mentally and physically ready, not trying to squeeze out one more big improvement.
If you feel flat, resist the urge to add hard work. Fresh beats sharp here.
blocks: If you feel flat, resist the urge to add hard work. Fresh beats sharp here.

sessions.title: 20-30 min easy run/walk if needed
sessions.purpose: Travel fresh, stay organised, and trust the build.
coachingNotes: Trust the work. The goal is not to dominate the route. The goal is to move through it safely and confidently.
If weather or body state make the route unsafe, changing the plan is a successful decision.
blocks: If weather or body state make the route unsafe, changing the plan is a successful decision.
